extra.message: User-controlled 'username' query parameter is concatenated directly into a Jinja2 template string via Jinja2.from_string('Welcome ' + username + '!'). The template is then rendered with .render(), which evaluates any Jinja2 template expressions in the user input. An attacker can inject template expressions (e.g., {{ 7*7 }}, {{ config }}, or RCE payloads) which will be executed server-side, allowing arbitrary code execution.

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Templating Engine
CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code
CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code
